exercises.title: Forward-Secure Key Exchange
exercises.description: Implement a forward-secure key exchange protocol using ephemeral keys.
tasks: Generate ephemeral ML-KEM key pair per session
Derive session key from ephemeral KEM
Implement secure key deletion after use
exercises.expectedOutput: Forward-secure protocol with verified ephemeral key deletion
hints: Use mutable bytearray for deletable keys
Test that past sessions can't be decrypted
Consider memory barriers for secure deletion

exercises.title: Revocation Management System
exercises.description: Build a key revocation management system.
tasks: Support invalidity date for compromise timing
exercises.expectedOutput: Revocation management system with CRL and OCSP
hints: CRL entries need serial, date, and reason
OCSP responses include status and validity period
Invalidity date can be before revocation date

exercises.title: Incident Response Planner
exercises.description: Create an incident response planning tool for key compromise.
tasks: Define recovery phases (detect, contain, eradicate, recover)
Generate action items based on key type
exercises.expectedOutput: Incident response planner with status tracking
hints: CA compromise has different steps than entity key

exercises.title: Compromise Impact Calculator
exercises.description: Create a tool to assess the impact of different key compromises.
tasks: Score impact by key type and usage
hints: Signing keys affect integrity; KEM keys affect confidentiality
CA keys have broader impact than end-entity
